qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Admin API exposes middleware credential material without redaction
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding is an admin API configuration disclosure in pkg/api handler code, while the advisory is an HTTP/2 frame-parser panic in the golang.org/x/net server dependency; the vulnerable code and root cause do not overlap.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The only final qualifying finding concerns unauthenticated disclosure from Traefik's admin API serialization path, not the advisory's HTTP/2 frame-handling panic path.
evidence: The advisory summary states that HTTP/2 frames can cause a running Traefik server to panic.
The fixed Traefik releases v2.11.40 and v3.6.10 attribute the advisory fix to bumping golang.org/x/net to v0.51.0 in the server area.

The upstream golang.org/x/net v0.50.0...v0.51.0 diff includes an http2/frame.go fix in typeFrameParser to avoid nil panic on unassigned frame types, whereas the finding's cited code locations are pkg/api/handler.go and pkg/api/handler_http.go for admin API JSON exposure.
